raw_text: 1. Truly my soul waiteth upon God: from him cometh my salvation. 2. He only is my rock and my salvation; he is my defence; I shall not be greatly moved. 3. How long will ye imagine mischief against a man? ye shall be slain all of you: as a bowing wall shall ye be, and as a tottering fence. 4. They only consult to cast him down from his excellency: they delight in lies: they bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah. 5. My soul, wait thou only upon God; for my expectation is from him. 6. He only is my rock and my salvation: he is my defence; I shall not be moved. 7. In God is my salvation and my glory: the rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God. 8. Trust in him at all times; ye people, pour out your heart before him: God is a refuge for us. Selah. 9. Surely men of low degree are vanity, and men of high degree are a lie: to be laid in the balance, they are altogether lighter than vanity. 10. Trust not in oppression, and become not vain in robbery: if riches increase, set not your heart upon them. 11. God hath spoken once; twice have I heard this; that power belongeth unto God. 12. Also unto thee, O Lord, belongeth mercy: for thou renderest to every man according to his work.
distilled_principles: Place ultimate trust in transcendent values rather than human power structures or material wealth
Recognize that all human hierarchies and material possessions are ultimately ephemeral and unreliable
Maintain inner stability through anchoring identity in unchanging principles rather than circumstances
Reject unethical means of gain even when facing opposition or hardship
Understand that moral actions have ultimate consequences beyond immediate appearances
ethics_reasoning: The passage strongly emphasizes justice through its teaching about divine judgment according to deeds and rejection of oppression. It promotes virtue ethics through modeling trust, patience, and rejection of ill-gotten gain. The deontological elements appear in implicit duties to avoid lies, oppression, and robbery, while commonsense morality underlies the warnings against trusting in vanity.
